title: feat(ui): design-system upgrade + alpha showcase
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
## Summary
- Bumps `@pollinations/ui` to `0.1.0-alpha.0` and adds the new primitives, model selector, and app-user composition.
- Reworks `react.pollinations.ai` around Models, Primitives, and Compositions, while keeping the exhaustive showcase at `?view=showcase`.
- Bundles wallet styles into the main UI stylesheet and updates enter/model-monitor consumers for the token-based modality themes.
- Adds an alpha stability warning plus focused tests for file partitioning and modality theme contracts.

## Validation
- `npm test --workspace @pollinations/ui`
- `npm run typecheck --workspace @pollinations/ui`
- `npm run build --workspace @pollinations/ui`
- `npm run typecheck --workspace enter.pollinations.ai`
- `npm run build --prefix apps/react`
- `npm run build --prefix apps/model-monitor`
- `npm run build:frontend --prefix enter.pollinations.ai`
- `npx biome check ...`

gist.headline: Fresh coat of pixels
gist.blurb: We just shipped a major upgrade to our UI design system. Expect smoother model selection, shiny new components, and a more cohesive look across the ecosystem.
gist.summary: Upgraded @pollinations/ui to 0.1.0-alpha.0, introducing a new model selector, app-user compositions, and fresh primitives including FileUpload and Markdown.
gist.impact: Developers using our React library get a richer toolkit, while users will see a cleaner, unified interface across the dashboard and model monitor.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee and the Monitor Robot organizing glowing UI building blocks (buttons, sliders) on a workbench, representing the new design system primitives and components.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly the bee wearing a tiny tool belt, organizing glowing UI building blocks on a wooden workbench. A chunky Monitor Robot stands nearby, its screen displaying a neat layout of buttons and sliders. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.
